headline: Local councillors still appointed, not elected, after 48 years
context: Malaysia suspended local government elections in 1976 and has not restored them, making it one of the only democracies where municipal councillors are appointed by state governments.

cards.big: 48 years without local elections — councillors answer to politicians, not residents
cards.sub: Malaysia suspended local polls in 1976 and never brought them back

cards.big: Local Government Act 1976 Section 15 gives state authority full appointment power
cards.sub: No constitutional barrier exists — restoration requires only political will

cards.big: Local governments collect RM8.9 billion annually in assessment taxes
cards.sub: Taxation without direct representation at the level closest to citizens

cards.big: Indonesia restored local elections in 2004, Thailand in 1994
cards.sub: Malaysia lags behind regional neighbours in grassroots democratic governance

cards.big: Without local elections, the third tier of government is just patronage with a budget
Is Malaysia ready to trust its citizens with local government elections?
